w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: In alignment with program policy, CTOS has developed a template for a mandatory artifact that must be produced for each application release
: the Container Scan Report (CSR). The CSR
ensures that all project team members have awareness of the state of security for release candidates,
provides security context for Technical Project Managers (TPMs) to make
go/no-go decisions on planned releases, and enables greater program visibility into cybersecurity practices.
The following subsections provide additional context to explain roles and responsibilities of CTOS project teams by functional role, as well as how
the CSR integrates with existing software development lifecycle (SDLC) processes
The Technical Project Manager (TPM) bares the responsibility and is accountable for ensuring that a CSR is generated
reporting directory for each application release. The TPM also has the authority to decide which project team member is responsible for assisting with the creation of the CSR
, though it is advised that the TPM designates this task to a DevOps Engineer supporting the project.
While a DevOps Engineer may perform the tasks required to populate the CSR, the TPM must be the individual who
CSR to the central reporting directory.
information that describes the application release, as well as the security state for all microservices supporting the application.
Scan result details must even be provided for microservices that have not been modified for the release scope.
The CSR must be produced, reviewed by the TPM, and submitted to the central reporting repository
This ensures that the TPM has information available to make informed decisions for the release processes with inclusion of security context.
By navigating to the Documents section of the site, TPMs will be able to locate a folder titled
serves as the central reporting repository for completed CSRs.
Please note that the NCI CBIIT CTOS Project Management SharePoint site also has a Microsoft Teams workspace if
The central reporting repository uses a folder structure to organize
scan reports by program and by project. TPMs are responsible for ensuring the timely upload of the CSR in the appropriate project directories. TPMs are
encouraged to create new directories for new projects as necessary.
Each scan report that is submitted to the central reporting repository must follow a common naming convention. The common naming convention is expressed as follows:
Cancer Data Service (CDS) application had a release
planned for January 01, 2025, and the release is version 2.4.0, then the file would be titled
Updated the “Security Scan Findings by Microservice” table to include an optional
column for Jira ticket number references.
The name of the Technical Project Manager
The date the report was produced
The version of the application planned for release
